steps.title: Read the y-intercept of the line of best fit for data set A
steps.content: Extend the line of best fit on the scatterplot to $x = 0$. The line crosses the $y$-axis at approximately $y \approx 53.75$ for data set A.

steps.title: Apply the shift to find the y-intercept for data set B
steps.content:
Data set B is formed by subtracting 11 from every $y$-value in data set A. Shifting all points down by a constant shifts the line of best fit down by the same amount, so the new $y$-intercept is:
$$b_B = 53.75 - 11 = 42.75$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
